title: HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS HOUSING SUPPORTS RENTAL ASSISTANCE - 211info
text: UMPQUA HEALTH ALLIANCE HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS HOUSING SUPPORTS RENTAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Health-related social needs (HRSN) are basic needs that affect a person's health. These benefits help members who qualify with basic needs, including rent payment assistance and utility payment assistance, tenant support, and storage fees. Members can only receive HRSN housing services once per lifetime. Eligibility: Umpqua Health Alliance members who meet all of the following: -Age 65 or older, children age 0-5, pregnant or gave birth in the past 12 months, OR anyone with a qualifying health condition -Have a household income 30% or less of the average yearly income where the applicant lives -Need support staying in housing -Have a lease or written rental agreement -Lack resources to prevent homelessness Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (541) 229-4842 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 229-4842 Main phone Main phone (541) 229-4842 TTY/TTD (541) 440-6304 Toll-free (888) 877-6304 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call, email, fax, or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: -Medicaid ID number, Proof of income, Rental/lease agreement LANGUAGES: Arabic, Chinese, English, Spanish, Russian, Vietnamese, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Douglas County Services: Manufactured/Mobile Home Lot Fee Payment Assistance * Medicaid Recipients Rent Payment Assistance * Medicaid Recipients Other Services Offered At This Location: FOOD - HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS NUTRITION SUPPORTS HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION HOUSING/SHELTER - FLEXIBLE SERVICES HOUSING/SHELTER - HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS HOUSING SUPPORTS HOME MODIFICATION MATERIAL GOODS - HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS CLIMATE SUPPORTS TRANSPORTATION - MEDICAL TRANSPORTATION MANAGEMENT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
